ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Rufflet via official Pokemon TCG database listing (card no. 152/191, Surging Sparks sv8) and Bulbapedia species entry for National Pokedex no. 627. Internal Pokemon IP — no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Rufflet is the Eaglet Pokemon, National Pokedex no. 627, a Normal/Flying type introduced in Generation 5 (Unova, Black/White, 2010). It is a male-only species and was version-exclusive to Pokemon White in the original games (Black featured the counterpart Vullaby/Mandibuzz pair). Its design is based on the bald eagle chick, with color scheme referencing the US flag; the single head plume draws from Native American eagle-feather imagery.
sources: GamesRadar: Pokemon Black and White Pokedex — Rufflet, Braviary, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Rufflet's Pokedex entries emphasize it provokes opponents significantly larger than itself, including its own parents, as a growth-through-adversity strategy. The more it is defeated and injured, the more powerful it becomes — a motif that carries through the entire Braviary evolution line.

A second evolution path exists via Hisuian Braviary, a Psychic/Flying regional form introduced in Pokemon Legends: Arceus (2022). Hisuian Braviary cannot be obtained by evolving Rufflet in Paldea; it requires transfer from Legends: Arceus through Pokemon HOME. Standard Braviary (Normal/Flying) evolves at level 54.
sources: GameRant: How to Get Hisuian Braviary in Scarlet/Violet, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: In the Surging Sparks set (released November 8, 2024), Rufflet is a 70 HP Basic Colorless-type card with a single-energy Flap attack dealing 30 damage. Its competitive function is as a setup piece for a Braviary evolution line rather than a standalone attacker.

related_cards.name: Hisuian Braviary
related_cards.note: Alternate regional evolution via Legends: Arceus

related_cards.name: Vullaby
related_cards.note: Version counterpart in Pokemon Black — thematic pair with Rufflet/Braviary
